intro:
A random name picker selects a random name from a list you provide, making fair selection easy for giveaways, classroom participation, team assignments, and any situation where you need an impartial random choice from a group of people. Instead of awkward manual draws or bias-prone selections, a random name picker delivers instant, transparent results.

Teachers use random name pickers to call on students fairly without showing favoritism. Contest organizers use them to select winners from entry lists. Managers use them to assign tasks or volunteers. Party hosts use them for games. The applications are endless whenever you need to randomly select from a group.

Our tool is simple, fast, and completely free. Paste a list of names, one per line, and click Pick. The selected name is highlighted dramatically, making it fun and suspenseful for group settings. You can also pick multiple names at once or remove picked names to avoid duplicates.
howItWorks: Enter names in the text area, one name per line. Set how many names you want to pick. Click Pick Random Name and the tool randomly selects from your list using a cryptographically random algorithm. The selected name(s) are announced clearly. You can remove picked names and repeat for multiple rounds.
interpretationGuide: Each name in your list has an equal probability of being selected. The selection is random and unbiased — no name has a higher or lower chance than any other. For weighted selection where some names should appear more often, add them multiple times to the list.

faqs.question: How fair is the random selection?
faqs.answer: The selection uses a cryptographically random number generator, making it statistically unbiased. Each name has exactly equal probability of being selected, with no patterns or favoritism.

faqs.question: Can I pick multiple names at once?
faqs.answer: Yes. Set the number of names to pick before clicking the button. The tool selects the specified number of unique names from your list, useful for selecting multiple winners or forming teams.

faqs.question: Can I prevent the same name from being picked twice?
faqs.answer: Yes. Enable the Remove after picking option to automatically remove selected names from the list. This ensures each name can only be picked once, perfect for sequential draws or eliminations.

faqs.question: How many names can I add?
faqs.answer: You can add hundreds of names to the list. The tool handles large lists efficiently, making it suitable for classroom rolls, contest entry lists, and large team assignments.

faqs.question: Can I save my name list?
faqs.answer: Your list may be saved in browser local storage for convenience so it persists between sessions. For permanent storage, keep your list in a text file or spreadsheet and paste it when needed.

faqs.question: Is this good for classroom use?
faqs.answer: Teachers find random name pickers extremely useful for calling on students fairly, assigning groups, selecting peer reviewers, and making classroom activities feel exciting and impartial to students.

faqs.question: Can I use this for giveaways?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Paste your entrant names and pick a winner. For transparent giveaways, consider recording your screen while picking so participants can verify the selection was truly random.

faqs.question: What if I have duplicate names?
faqs.answer: If the same name appears multiple times in your list, it has proportionally higher odds of selection. This can be intentional for weighted selection. If unintentional, remove duplicates before picking.

faqs.question: Can I use this for team building?
faqs.answer: Yes. Enter all participants, then pick groups of equal size sequentially to form random teams. Remove picked names after each pick to ensure everyone ends up on exactly one team.

faqs.question: How do I enter names from a spreadsheet?
faqs.answer: Copy a column of names from your spreadsheet, then paste them into the name picker. Most spreadsheet applications copy column data with each cell on a new line, which is exactly the format the picker expects.
